title: artrolobium ebracteatum
intro: Artrolobium ebracteatum, also known as the West Indian fish poison tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Artrolobium ebracteatum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tincture, infusion, or decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
